title: MongoDB Index Build and collMod Operations
dataSources: mongod log messages for `createIndexes`, `dropIndexes`, `collMod`, or profiler events
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder on mongod hosts
spl:
index=database sourcetype="mongodb:log"
| search "createIndexes" OR "dropIndexes" OR "collMod"
| rex field=_raw "ns:\s+(?<ns>[^\s]+)"
| stats earliest(_time) as started latest(_raw) as sample by ns, host
| sort -started
description: Index builds change performance characteristics and can throttle production workloads. Operators track createIndexes/dropIndexes/collMod events for correlation with latency regressions.
value: Speeds root-cause analysis when deployments coincide with long-running index builds on large collections.
implementation: Ensure mongod logs include command namespace. For Atlas, ship equivalent audit events. Pair with currentOp index builds panel. Filter routine automation via service account lookup.
visualization: Timeline (operations), Table (ns, host), Drill to raw sample.

references.title: MongoDB Index Builds on Populated Collections

knownFalsePositives: Maintenance work (ANALYZE, VACUUM, REINDEX, index builds) and ETL or large report jobs may show up as long-running or blocking — compare with the maintenance and batch schedule.
grandmaExplanation: We surface slow and blocking queries so we can fix the worst offenders first and keep applications and batch jobs within the response times we promise.
